content.summary: Layers multiple Noise TOPs at different scales, speeds, and blend modes to create rich, organic procedural textures. Each noise layer acts as an octave — large-scale noise provides broad structure while small-scale noise adds fine detail. Compositing them together with varying opacities produces complex textures suitable for backgrounds, displacement maps, or particle driving forces.

codeSnippets.label: Create a 3-octave layered noise setup
codeSnippets.code:
# Create three noise layers at different scales
base = op('/project1')

# Layer 1: Large-scale base
n1 = base.create(noiseTOP, 'noise_large')
n1.par.type = 'sparse'
n1.par.period = 500
n1.par.harmonics = 1
n1.par.translate1 = 'absTime.seconds * 0.02'

# Layer 2: Medium detail
n2 = base.create(noiseTOP, 'noise_medium')
n2.par.type = 'sparse'
n2.par.period = 150
n2.par.harmonics = 1
n2.par.translate1 = 'absTime.seconds * 0.05'

# Layer 3: Fine detail
n3 = base.create(noiseTOP, 'noise_fine')
n3.par.type = 'sparse'
n3.par.period = 40
n3.par.harmonics = 1
n3.par.translate1 = 'absTime.seconds * 0.1'

# Composite: add layers with decreasing amplitude
comp1 = base.create(compositeTOP, 'comp_layers1')
comp1.par.operand = 'add'
comp1.inputConnectors[0].connect(n1)
comp1.inputConnectors[1].connect(n2)

comp2 = base.create(compositeTOP, 'comp_layers2')
comp2.par.operand = 'add'
comp2.inputConnectors[0].connect(comp1)
comp2.inputConnectors[1].connect(n3)

# Level to normalize the combined range
lvl = base.create(levelTOP, 'level_normalize')
lvl.inputConnectors[0].connect(comp2)
lvl.par.brightness1 = 0.5
lvl.par.contrast = 1.5
codeSnippets.description: Creates three noise octaves at different spatial frequencies and speeds, composited together additively.

tips: Each octave should have roughly half the period and half the amplitude of the previous one for natural-looking fractal noise.
Animate each layer's translate at different speeds to create complex organic motion.
Use 'sparse' noise type for smoother results; 'random' for more chaotic textures.
Apply a Level TOP at the end to remap the combined range back to 0-1 and add contrast.
